name: Deep-Dish Chicken Pie
recipeIngredient: 3 stalks celery with leaves, cut up
1 refrigerated unbaked pie crust, room temperature
1/2 cup red bell pepper, cut into 1/2-inch pieces
recipeInstructions:
1. Place chicken in a large kettle with 5 cups water, celery, onion, bay leaf, salt, and pepper. Bring to a boil; reduce heat, cover, and simmer for 45 to 60 minutes until chicken is tender. Remove chicken and set aside.
2. Using a sieve lined with several layers of cheesecloth, strain broth into a large bowl. Discard vegetables and skim fat from the broth. Measure 1-1/2 cups of broth; reserve the remainder for another use.
3. When chicken is cool, pull meat from bones and cut into 1/2-inch cubes. Discard skin and bones.
4. Preheat oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit. In a large saucepan, melt butter over medium heat, add mushrooms, leeks, celery, and red pepper. Cook about 3 minutes until tender. Stir in flour, poultry seasoning, salt, and pepper. Add the broth and cream all at once; cook and stir until thickened and bubbly. Stir in chicken and peas.
5. Pour mixture into a round 1.5-quart casserole. Unfold pie crust and cut into a circle 2-inches larger in diameter than the top of the casserole. Brush edges of the casserole with egg white.
6. Place crust atop casserole; press edge under and flute to edge. Cut a 1-inch hole in the center of the crust to vent. Cut pastry scraps into decorative shapes; brush top of crust with egg white and lay on the scraps, brushing again with egg white.
7. Bake in a 400-degree oven for 30 to 40 minutes until the crust is golden brown. Serve immediately.
